blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Good judgment. It's foolish to ignore nine things just because you've obtained one out of ten. No matter the situation, you must never stop thinking. Everything in the world is determined by the volume of thought. Battles are no different.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Don't misunderstand. I will never forgive you.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Kekeke! If you've got spirit too, that's perfect. Shall I take a guess? You're the type who grows through real combat. A school that only teaches rigid things doesn't suit you. How about it? Won't you come with me? I'll make you the greatest mage.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Stop spouting nonsense. I respect the teachers here far more than a murderer like you.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Then tell me. What have you learned here? Photon cannons? Red wavelengths? If not that, did you at least get advice on the Immortal Function?
Shironé's eyes wavered slightly.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: How do you know about that……?
Arkane burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: This must be why people become teachers. Gathering clueless children and spouting nonsense to their heart's content.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: How rare. Truly rare. Is the Immortal Function an ordinary achievement? But child, there are terrifying monsters living in places you don't know. If you mingle with them energetically……
Arkane narrowed one eye and smiled.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Unlockers aren't that rare either.
Shironé swallowed dryly.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Just how many life-and-death battles must one cross to say Unlockers aren't rare? Regardless of skill, the accumulated experience was on a different level.
Individuality could be a variable that ignored objective strength, but it could also helplessly lose its power in the face of straightforward methods.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: You have nothing to learn in a school that ignores individuality and teaches only uniformity. You must sharpen your unique strengths. I can help you with that.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: I don't learn magic to defeat someone. Don't lump me together with you.
Arkane saw conflict in Shironé's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: You probably think everyone likes you now. But humans are naturally favorable to the extraordinary. It's not because they like you as a person. How long do you think you can keep interacting with such hypocrites?
Everyone listening frowned at Arkane's words.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: But Shironé remained silent.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Grow stronger. So they can never ignore you. Otherwise, they'll trample you. Do you think you can avoid conflict with pretty excuses when that happens?
Arkane held up three fingers.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Three years. I'll make you strong enough that no one on the continent can ignore you in that time. Do you think three years is too short? I don't. If we maximize your uniqueness, it's far from impossible.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: At the very least, it wasn't nonsense to Shironé. As an Unlocker, there was little benefit in staying at school and rejecting a Grand Mage's guidance. At best, it might make finding a job in magical society easier.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: You're right.
Iruki and Neid's faces darkened.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Shironé……

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: If it was the truth, there was no need to deny it. Unlockers held a unique position even in magical society, and the standardized education of magic schools had limits in nurturing true strength.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Perhaps you're right. But I still won't follow you. School isn't a place for me to fight—it's where my life is. Teachers, friends, a sense of belonging. I don't think you can give me any of that.
Arkane scowled.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Whatever tricks Alphaeus had used, his ability to brainwash students was impressive.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: You're being deceived. When you actually live your life, such things won't matter. If you weren't strong, you'd just be another ordinary kid at school. Proving your strength is the only thing that matters in life.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: If that's really all there is, then why are you doing this?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: You became a Grand Mage through such battles. What do you have left now? Everyone hates you. Aren't you lonely too? Because no one cares about you, this is the only way you can prove yourself.
Arkane's face reddened as he gritted his teeth.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: You're spouting nonsense. The strong are lonely by nature. The weak cling together out of weakness. Look at the current situation. Everyone is kneeling at my feet. I rule over them. This is what loneliness is.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: No. You're just a murderer no one cares about.
At the word 'murderer,' madness flashed in Arkane's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Hahaha! Perhaps. But do you know? I once had something called comrades. But he led everyone around him to ruin. That man is Alphaeus, the one you respect.
Shironé didn't believe it.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: Etela had told him the two had a grudge. The Alphaeus he knew would never lead anyone to ruin.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: I don't know what happened in the past, but I won't listen to you. Because you're a murderer. And because you're still killing now.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: A murderer. Would you say the same if Alphaeus… were the same as me?
Shironé's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: That's a lie. What do you know about the headmaster?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Kekeke! Of course I know. That brat was my disciple.
Shironé was speechless.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: What……?
Shironé was shocked.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: How could a dark magic user be his teacher?
Feeling the irony himself, Arkane looked up at the sky with a distant gaze.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: The events of that day felt as vivid as yesterday.
Suddenly remembering something, he turned to Etela.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Did Alphaeus ever marry?

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: No. He has lived alone his whole life, devoted to education.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: I see.
A fleeting sadness passed through Arkane's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: After 40 years of hating Alphaeus, the only thing he could pity was Elina's story.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: You may not know this, but Alphaeus was once married.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: To her knowledge, Alphaeus was unmarried. Even if he had married and divorced, as a union of noble families, it should have been recorded.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: But the headmaster is listed as unmarried in all records.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: So it was never recognized. But Alphaeus was married. They lived together for three years. Her name was Elina. A remarkable woman. She shone brighter and was more beautiful than anyone.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: What happened between the two of you? Was it something worth holding a grudge over for 50 years?

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: Back then, Alphaeus was a rising star, recognized for his photon theory. He even received the Gold Circle Award from the emperor—can you imagine his status? His position in the Thunder Magic Department was secured, and he set up a household with a woman from the Bastard family. He had everything the world could offer. But he had one worry.
Arkane tapped his head.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: His wife, Elina, was a bit… lacking in intelligence. About the level of a ten-year-old.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: I see.

blocks.speaker: Arkane
blocks.content: But Alphaeus didn't mind. He truly loved his wife. His love was never pity or pretense.
Arkane's memories traveled back 40 years.
A time when he ruled the world, and Alphaeus was hailed as a rising star in the magic world.

blocks.speaker: Narrator
blocks.content: This year's Gold Circle Award goes to… Mirhi Alphaeus!
A fanfare echoed through the grand hall.
As multicolored confetti rained from the ceiling, Alphaeus shed tears of joy.

blocks.speaker: Narrator
blocks.content: The Gold Circle Award, given to the mage with the year's greatest achievement. Winning it guaranteed entry into the Thunder Magic Department and support from numerous noble families.

blocks.speaker: Narrator
blocks.content: Congratulations, Alphaeus. I knew you'd win.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I'll be counting on you. You won't forget your fellow students now that you're successful, will you?
Dozens of mages swarmed around Alphaeus.

blocks.speaker: Narrator
blocks.content: With his stock set to rise endlessly, they wanted to get on his good side early.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Hmph. He got famous by catching one idiot. With the Mirhi and Bastard families combined, how could anyone compete?

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Exactly. A poisoned chalice, truly. But what can we do? The results are what they are. We'll have to watch our tongues around Alphaeus from now on.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Sarof knew it too. As long as he couldn't leave magical society, befriending Alphaeus was the best course.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: What now, Sarof? It'll be awkward if we meet alone. Should we sneak in now and make peace?

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: No, I'm not in the mood. You go ahead if you want.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: I feel the same. Let's do it later. Two is better than one. Want to grab a drink?

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Yeah. I don't want to stay here any longer.
Sarof left the royal palace with his friends.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: He had been a finalist for the Gold Circle Award, but no one paid attention to the loser.
Clump burst into hearty laughter, dragging Alphaeus through the crowd.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Hahaha! You did it! My friend won the Gold Circle! I can't believe it!

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Ow! That hurts!
Clump's celebration didn't stop.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: You were so annoying, acting all smart, but turns out you're a genius! You did it, man!

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Ah, I can't go right away. I need to stop by somewhere first.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: What? If the guest of honor leaves, what's the point of toasting?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I need to go home. I want to give the medal to my wife.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Even Clump couldn't argue with that.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Though married just a year ago, due to opposition from both families, Elina lived in a small newlywed home in the commoners' district without any support.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: It started with the Mirhi family. They couldn't accept their son, sent abroad for studies, marrying a 'foolish' woman.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: In response, the Bastard family refused to back down. Even among first-class nobles, there was a clear difference between regional and capital families, so they wouldn't yield even if their daughter was a bit slow.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: In the end, Alphaeus and Elina had no choice but to hold an unofficial wedding with only friends in attendance.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: I see. The wife. Why don't you bring her along?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: No. What wife would like seeing her husband drunk? I'll just make a quick stop.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: You've got tact, huh? Then hurry back.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I'll be quick. Go ahead to the bar!
Alphaeus fled the palace like a man chased.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I did it! I really did it!
He had won the kingdom's most prestigious award.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: With such an achievement, even the Mirhi family would have to accept him, making a formal marriage ceremony with Elina no longer a dream.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Honey, I'm home.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: You're back? Have you eaten?
